name: validate_data_safety
description: Call BEFORE your agent stores, transmits, logs, or passes any data payload to another system. AI-powered classification for GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and CCPA. Returns machine-readable verdict: SAFE_TO_PROCESS, REDACT_BEFORE_PASSING, DO_NOT_STORE, or ESCALATE with detected data categories.

arguments.name: payload
arguments.description: The data payload to classify. Any string, JSON, form data, API response, or text content. Analysed in memory and immediately discarded.

arguments.name: context
arguments.description: What your agent is about to do with this data (e.g. write to database, send to API). Improves verdict accuracy.

arguments.name: data_origin_ip
arguments.description: IP address of the data subject for jurisdiction detection (GDPR if EU, CCPA if US).

arguments.name: jurisdiction
arguments.description: Override jurisdiction if known (EU, US, UK, CA, AU).

name: get_safety_report
description: BATCH mode: classify up to 50 data payloads with full AI reasoning per payload in one call. AUDIT mode: generate structured compliance documentation for a dataset description. Returns per-payload verdict array with aggregate risk summary and triggered regulatory frameworks.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: BATCH for classifying up to 50 payloads; AUDIT for generating compliance report documentation

arguments.name: payloads
arguments.description: Array of data payloads to classify. Required for BATCH mode. Maximum 50.

arguments.name: dataset_description
arguments.description: Description of the dataset for AUDIT mode.

arguments.name: context
arguments.description: What will be done with this data. Improves verdict accuracy.

name: validate_data_safety_lite
description: Pattern-detection only, no AI call. Returns SAFE_TO_PROCESS or REVIEW_REQUIRED in under 100ms at ~70% lower token cost than validate_data_safety. Use when budget-constrained or for high-volume pre-screening before committing to full AI classification.

arguments.name: payload
arguments.description: The data payload to screen for sensitive patterns.

arguments.name: context
arguments.description: Optional: what your agent plans to do with this data.
